name: mcstatus

metadata.name: mcstatus
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A library to query Minecraft Servers for their status and capabilities.

documentation: # MCStatus [![discord chat](https://img.shields.io/discord/936788458939224094.svg?logo=Discord)](https://discord.gg/C2wX7zduxC) ![supported python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/mcstatus.svg) [![current PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/mcstatus.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/mcstatus/) [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/readthedocs/mcstatus?label=Docs)](https://mcstatus.readthedocs.io/) [![Validation](https://github.com/py-mine/mcstatus/actions/workflows/validation.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/py-mine/mcstatus/actions/workflows/validation.yml) [![Tox test](https://github.com/py-mine/mcstatus/actions/workflows/tox-test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/py-mine/mcstatus/actions/workflows/tox-test.yml) Mcstatus provides an API and command line script to fetch publicly available data from Minecraft servers. Specifically, mcstatus retrieves data by using these protocols: [Server List Ping](https://wiki.vg/Server_List_Ping) and [Query](https://wiki.vg/Query). Because of mcstatus, you do not need to fully understand those protocols and can instead skip straight to retrieving minecraft server data quickly in your own programs. ## Installation Mcstatus is available on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/mcstatus/), and can be installed trivially with: ```bash python3 -m pip install mcstatus ``` ## Usage ### Python API #### Java Edition ```python from mcstatus import JavaServer # You can pass the same address you'd enter into the address field in minecraft into the 'lookup' function # If you know the host and port, you may skip this and use JavaServer("example.org", 1234) server = JavaServer.lookup("example.org:1234") # 'status' is supported by all Minecraft servers that are version 1.7 or higher. # Don't expect the player list to always be complete, because many servers run # plugins that hide this information or limit the number of players returned or even # alter this list to contain fake players for purposes of having a custom message here. status = server.status() print(f"The server has {status.players.online} player(s) online and replied in {status.latency} ms") # 'ping' is supported by all Minecraft servers that are version 1.7 or higher. # It is included in a 'status' call, but is also exposed separate if you do not require the additional info. latency = server.ping() print(f"The server replied in {latency} ms") # 'query' has to be enabled in a server's server.properties file! # It may give more information than a ping, such as a full player list or mod information. query = server.query() print(f"The server has the following players online: {', '.join(query.players.names)}") ``` #### Bedrock Edition ```python from mcstatus import BedrockServer # You can pass the same address you'd enter into the address field in minecraft into the 'lookup' function # If you know the host and port, you may skip this and use BedrockServer("example.org", 19132) server = BedrockServer.lookup("example.org:19132") # 'status' is the only feature that is supported by Bedrock at this time. # In this case status includes players.online, latency, motd, map, gamemode, and players.max. (ex: status.gamemode) status = server.status() print(f"The server has {status.players.online} players online and replied in {status.latency} ms") ``` See the [documentation](https://mcstatus.readthedocs.io) to find what you can do with our library! ### Command Line Interface This only works with Java servers; Bedrock is not yet supported. Use `mcstatus -h` to see helpful information on how to use this script. ## License Mcstatus is licensed under the Apache 2.0 license. See LICENSE for full text.

functions.name: mcstatus.utils.retry
documentation:
Decorator that re-runs given function `tries` times if error occurs.

The amount of tries will either be the value given to the decorator,
or if tries is present in keyword arguments on function call, this
specified value will take precedence.

If the function fails even after all the retries, raise the last
exception that the function raised.

:::{note}
Even if the previous failures caused a different exception, this will only raise the last one.
:::

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.get_unused_elements
documentation:
Get indices of all items which are unused and can be safely removed from the MOTD.

This is a wrapper method around several unused item collection methods.

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.squash_nearby_strings
documentation:
Squash duplicate strings together.

Note that this function doesn't create a copy of passed array, it modifies it.
This is what those typevars are for in the function signature.

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.get_double_items
documentation:
Get indices of all doubled items that can be removed.

Removes any items that are followed by an item of the same kind (compared using `__eq__`).

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.get_double_colors
documentation:
Get indices of all doubled color items.

As colors (obviously) override each other, we only ever care about the last one, ignore
the previous ones. (for example: specifying red color, then orange, then yellow, then some text
will just result in yellow text)

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.get_formatting_before_color
documentation:
Obtain indices of all unused formatting items before colors.

Colors override any formatting before them, meaning we only ever care about the color, and can
ignore all formatting before it. (For example: specifying bold formatting, then italic, then yellow,
will just result in yellow text.)

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.get_empty_text
documentation:
Get indices of all empty text items.

Empty strings in motd serve no purpose and can be marked for removal.

functions.name: mcstatus.motd.simplifies.get_end_non_text
documentation:
Get indices of all trailing items, found after the last text component.

Any color/formatting items only make sense when they apply to some text.
If there are some at the end, after the last text, they're pointless and
can be removed.

classes.name: mcstatus.server.MCServer

methods.name: mcstatus.server.MCServer.__init__
documentation:
---
param host: The host/ip of the minecraft server.
param port: The port that the server is on.
param timeout: The timeout in seconds before failing to connect.
---

methods.name: mcstatus.server.MCServer.lookup
documentation:
Mimics minecraft's server address field.

```
param address

The address of the Minecraft server, like example.com:19132

param timeout

The timeout in seconds before failing to connect.
```

Base abstract class for a general minecraft server.

This class only contains the basic logic shared across both java and bedrock versions,
it doesn't include any version specific settings and it can't be used to make any requests.

classes.name: mcstatus.server.JavaServer

methods.name: mcstatus.server.JavaServer.lookup
documentation:
Mimics minecraft's server address field.

With Java servers, on top of just parsing the address, we also check the
DNS records for an SRV record that points to the server, which is the same
behavior as with minecraft's server address field for Java. This DNS record
resolution is happening synchronously (see {meth}`.async_lookup`).

```
param address

The address of the Minecraft server, like example.com:25565.

param timeout

The timeout in seconds before failing to connect.
```

methods.name: mcstatus.server.JavaServer.async_lookup
documentation:
Asynchronous alternative to {meth}`.lookup`.

For more details, check the {meth}`JavaServer.lookup() <.lookup>` docstring.

classes.name: mcstatus.motd.components.Formatting
documentation:
Enum for Formatting codes.

See [Minecraft wiki](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Formatting_codes#Formatting_codes)
for more info.

:::{note}
{attr}`.STRIKETHROUGH` and {attr}`.UNDERLINED` don't work on Bedrock, which our parser
doesn't keep it in mind. See [MCPE-41729](https://bugs.mojang.com/browse/MCPE-41729).
:::

classes.name: mcstatus.motd.components.TranslationTag
documentation:
Represents a `translate` field in server's answer.

This just exists, but is completely ignored by our transformers.
You can find translation tags in {attr}`Motd.parsed <mcstatus.motd.Motd.parsed>` attribute.

:::{seealso}
[Minecraft's wiki.](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Raw_JSON_text_format#Translated_Text)
:::

classes.name: mcstatus.motd.transformers.BaseTransformer
documentation:
Base motd transformer class.

Motd transformer is responsible for providing a way to generate an alternative representation
of motd, such as one that is able to be printed in the terminal.

classes.name: mcstatus.motd.transformers.NothingTransformer
documentation:
Transformer that transforms all elements into empty strings.

This transformer acts as a base for other transformers with string result type.

classes.name: mcstatus.motd.transformers.HtmlTransformer
documentation:
Formatter for HTML variant of a MOTD.

:::{warning}
You should implement obfuscated CSS class yourself (name - `obfuscated`).
See [this answer](https://stackoverflow.com/a/30313558) as example.
:::

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.transformers.AnsiTransformer.ansi_color
documentation: Transform RGB color to ANSI color code.

class_variables.name: mcstatus.status_response.JavaStatusPlayers.sample
documentation:
List of players, who are online. If server didn't provide this, it will be {obj}`None`.

Actually, this is what appears when you hover over the slot count on the multiplayer screen.

:::{note}
It's often empty or even contains some advertisement, because the specific server implementations or plugins can
disable providing this information or even change it to something custom.

There is nothing that `mcstatus` can to do here if the player sample was modified/disabled like this.
:::
Class for storing information about players on the server.

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.Motd._parse_color
documentation: Parse a color string.

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.Motd.simplify
documentation:
Create new MOTD without unused elements.

After parsing, the MOTD may contain some unused elements, like empty strings, or formattings/colors
that don't apply to anything. This method is responsible for creating a new motd with all such elements
removed, providing a much cleaner representation.

:returns: New simplified MOTD, with any unused elements removed.

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.Motd.to_plain
documentation:
Get plain text from a MOTD, without any colors/formatting.

This is just a shortcut to {class}`~mcstatus.motd.transformers.PlainTransformer`.

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.Motd.to_minecraft
documentation:
Get Minecraft variant from a MOTD.

This is just a shortcut to {class}`~mcstatus.motd.transformers.MinecraftTransformer`.

:::{note}
This will always use `§`, even if in original MOTD used `&`.
:::

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.Motd.to_html
documentation:
Get HTML from a MOTD.

This is just a shortcut to {class}`~mcstatus.motd.transformers.HtmlTransformer`.

methods.name: mcstatus.motd.Motd.to_ansi
documentation:
Get ANSI variant from a MOTD.

This is just a shortcut to {class}`~mcstatus.motd.transformers.AnsiTransformer`.

:::{note}
We support only ANSI 24 bit colors, please implement your own transformer if you need other standards.
:::

:::{seealso}
<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code>
:::

functions.name: mcstatus.address._valid_urlparse
documentation:
Parses a string address like 127.0.0.1:25565 into host and port parts

If the address doesn't have a specified port, None will be returned instead.

```
raises ValueError

Unable to resolve hostname of given address
```

functions.name: mcstatus.address.minecraft_srv_address_lookup
documentation:
Lookup the SRV record for a Minecraft server.

Firstly it parses the address, if it doesn't include port, tries SRV record, and if it's not there,
falls back on `default_port`.

This function essentially mimics the address field of a Minecraft Java server. It expects an address like
`192.168.0.100:25565`, if this address does contain a port, it will simply use it. If it doesn't, it will try
to perform an SRV lookup, which if found, will contain the info on which port to use. If there's no SRV record,
this will fall back to the given `default_port`.

```
param address

The same address which would be used in minecraft's server address field.
Can look like: 127.0.0.1, or 192.168.0.100:12345, or mc.hypixel.net, or example.com:12345.

param lifetime

How many seconds a query should run before timing out.
Default value for this is inherited from .

raises ValueError

Either the address isn't valid and can't be parsed,
or it lacks a port, SRV record isn't present, and default_port wasn't specified.
```

functions.name: mcstatus.address.async_minecraft_srv_address_lookup
documentation: Just an async alternative to {func}`.minecraft_srv_address_lookup`, check it for more details.

classes.name: mcstatus.address._AddressBase
documentation:
Intermediate NamedTuple class representing an address.

We can't extend this class directly, since NamedTuples are slotted and
read-only, however child classes can extend __new__, allowing us do some
needed processing on child classes derived from this base class.

classes.name: mcstatus.address.Address

methods.name: mcstatus.address.Address.from_tuple
documentation: Construct the class from a regular tuple of `(host, port)`, commonly used for addresses.

methods.name: mcstatus.address.Address.from_path
documentation:
Construct the class from a {class}`~pathlib.Path` object.

If path has a port specified, use it, if not fall back to `default_port` kwarg.
In case `default_port` isn't provided and port wasn't specified, raise {exc}`ValueError`.

methods.name: mcstatus.address.Address.parse_address
documentation:
Parses a string address like `127.0.0.1:25565` into {attr}`.host` and {attr}`.port` parts.

If the address has a port specified, use it, if not, fall back to `default_port` kwarg.

```
raises ValueError

Either the address isn't valid and can't be parsed,
or it lacks a port and default_port wasn't specified.
```

methods.name: mcstatus.address.Address.resolve_ip
documentation:
Resolves a hostname's A record into an IP address.

If the host is already an IP, this resolving is skipped
and host is returned directly.

```
param lifetime

How many seconds a query should run before timing out.
Default value for this is inherited from .

raises dns.exception.DNSException

One of the exceptions possibly raised by .
Most notably this will be  and 
```

methods.name: mcstatus.address.Address.async_resolve_ip
documentation:
Resolves a hostname's A record into an IP address.

See the docstring for {meth}`.resolve_ip` for further info. This function is purely
an async alternative to it.

Extension of a {class}`~typing.NamedTuple` of {attr}`.host` and {attr}`.port`, for storing addresses.

This class inherits from {class}`tuple`, and is fully compatible with all functions
which require pure `(host, port)` address tuples, but on top of that, it includes
some neat functionalities, such as validity ensuring, alternative constructors
for easy quick creation and methods handling IP resolving.

:::{note}
The class is not a part of a Public API, but attributes {attr}`host` and {attr}`port` are a part of Public API.
:::

functions.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.ip_type
documentation:
Determinate what IP version is.

```
param address

A string or integer, the IP address. Either IPv4 or IPv6 addresses may be supplied.
Integers less than 2**32 will be considered to be IPv4 by default.

return

4 or 6 if the IP is IPv4 or IPv6, respectively.  if the IP is invalid.
```

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write
documentation: Write data to `self`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync._pack
documentation: Pack data in with format in big-endian mode.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_varint
documentation:
Write varint with value `value` to `self`.

```
param value

Maximum is 2 ** 31 - 1, minimum is -(2 ** 31).

raises ValueError

If value is out of range.
```

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_varlong
documentation:
Write varlong with value `value` to `self`.

```
param value

Maximum is 2 ** 63 - 1, minimum is -(2 ** 63).

raises ValueError

If value is out of range.
```

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_utf
documentation: Write varint of length of `value` up to 32767 bytes, then write `value` encoded with `UTF-8`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_ascii
documentation: Write value encoded with `ISO-8859-1`, then write an additional `0x00` at the end.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_short
documentation: Write 2 bytes for value `-32768 - 32767`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_ushort
documentation: Write 2 bytes for value `0 - 65535 (2 ** 16 - 1)`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_int
documentation: Write 4 bytes for value `-2147483648 - 2147483647`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_uint
documentation: Write 4 bytes for value `0 - 4294967295 (2 ** 32 - 1)`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_bool
documentation: Write 1 byte for boolean `True` or `False`

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseWriteSync.write_buffer
documentation: Flush buffer, then write a varint of the length of the buffer's data, then write buffer data.
Base synchronous write class

documentation: Read length bytes from `self`, and return a byte array.

documentation: Unpack data as bytes with format in big-endian.

documentation:

```
param value

Maximum is 2 ** 31 - 1, minimum is -(2 ** 31).

```

documentation:
Read varlong from `self` and return it.

```
param value

Maximum is 2 ** 63 - 1, minimum is -(2 ** 63).

raises IOError

If varint received is out of range.
```

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseReadSync.read_utf
documentation: Read up to 32767 bytes by reading a varint, then decode bytes as `UTF-8`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseReadSync.read_ascii
documentation: Read `self` until last value is not zero, then return that decoded with `ISO-8859-1`

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseReadSync.read_short
documentation: Return `-32768 - 32767`. Read 2 bytes.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseReadSync.read_ushort
documentation: Return `0 - 65535 (2 ** 16 - 1)`. Read 2 bytes.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseReadSync.read_bool
documentation: Return `True` or `False`. Read 1 byte.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.BaseReadSync.read_buffer
documentation: Read a varint for length, then return a new connection from length read bytes.

Base Connection class. Implements flush, receive, and remaining.

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection.read
documentation: Return {attr}`.received` up to length bytes, then cut received up to that point.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection.write
documentation: Extend {attr}`.sent` from `data`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection.receive
documentation: Extend {attr}`.received` with `data`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection.remaining
documentation: Return length of {attr}`.received`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection.flush
documentation: Return {attr}`.sent`, also clears {attr}`.sent`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.Connection.copy
documentation: Return a copy of ``self``
Base connection class.

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.SocketConnection

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.SocketConnection.close
documentation: Close {attr}`.socket`.
Socket connection.

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.TCPSocketConnection

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.TCPSocketConnection.read
documentation: Return length bytes read from {attr}`.socket`. Raises {exc}`IOError` when server doesn't respond.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.TCPSocketConnection.write
documentation: Send data on {attr}`.socket`.
TCP Connection to address. Timeout defaults to 3 seconds.

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.UDPSocketConnection

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.UDPSocketConnection.read
documentation: Return up to {meth}`.remaining` bytes. Length does nothing here.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.UDPSocketConnection.write
documentation: Use {attr}`.socket` to send data to {attr}`.addr`.
UDP Connection class

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.TCPAsyncSocketConnection

documentation: Use {mod}`asyncio` to open a connection to address. Timeout is in seconds.

documentation: Read up to `length` bytes from {attr}`.reader`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.TCPAsyncSocketConnection.write
documentation: Write data to {attr}`.writer`.

methods.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.TCPAsyncSocketConnection.close
documentation: Close {attr}`.writer`.
Asynchronous TCP Connection class

classes.name: mcstatus.protocol.connection.UDPAsyncSocketConnection
